## Business Context

### Release Plan (3 releases)
#### Release 1: Homeowner Core Services & Asset Digitalization
This initial release empowers homeowners to seamlessly request and track home repair services from verified vendors. It also introduces the foundational capability for owners to digitalize their property assets by uploading photos and creating detailed profiles, setting the stage for a comprehensive property record. Basic dispute resolution and internal platform monitoring capabilities are also established to ensure a stable and reliable service from day one.

**Rationale:** This release delivers the minimum viable product by addressing the most immediate pain point for homeowners: finding reliable help for repairs. It establishes the core user experience and begins building out the digital asset registry, which is a fundamental platform component. Features in subsequent releases will depend on these core service request and asset management capabilities.

**Journeys:** UJ-1, UJ-2, UJ-9, UJ-8, UJ-12
**Domains:** DOM-PROPERTY-ASSETS, DOM-WORK-ORDER, DOM-VENDOR-MGMT, DOM-DIGITAL-ASSETS, DOM-NOTIFICATION, DOM-SUPPORT

#### Release 2: Landlord & Vendor Operations Expansion
Building on Release 1, this phase expands the platform's utility to landlords and investors, allowing them to compare vendor quotes, manage portfolio reporting across multiple units, and escalate vendor disputes. Crucially, it introduces comprehensive onboarding and management tools for service providers, enabling contractors to join the platform, complete field jobs, invoice clients, and manage their compliance dashboard. This establishes the critical supply side of the marketplace.

**Rationale:** After establishing core homeowner demand in Release 1, Release 2 focuses on scaling the platform by onboarding the supply side (vendors) and catering to the more complex needs of landlords and investors. Vendor management, job completion, and compliance are essential for a functioning marketplace, and these capabilities directly leverage the service request flows initiated in Release 1.

**Journeys:** UJ-3, UJ-10, UJ-14, UJ-11, UJ-4, UJ-5
**Domains:** DOM-VENDOR-MGMT, DOM-FINANCE, DOM-COMPLIANCE, DOM-REPORTING, DOM-VERIFICATION, DOM-DISPATCH

#### Release 3: Community Association Governance & Finance
This final phase introduces advanced functionality tailored for community associations. It enables association managers to process resident maintenance requests, board members to review budgets, vote on actions, and access essential association documents. Additionally, it streamlines financial operations by allowing association managers to reconcile resident dues, providing a comprehensive solution for HOA governance and financial management within the Hestami ecosystem.

**Rationale:** Release 3 addresses the most complex and specialized segment of the real property ecosystem: community association management. These features depend on the maturity of core service request and financial transaction capabilities established in earlier releases. Introducing governance, complex financial reconciliation, and document management after the individual property owner and vendor workflows are solidified ensures a robust foundation.

**Journeys:** UJ-6, UJ-7, UJ-13, UJ-15
**Domains:** DOM-GOVERNANCE, DOM-FINANCE, DOM-COMMUNITY, DOM-CONTRACTS

### Intent Lens
**Lens:** product  
**Confidence:** 0.95  
**Rationale:** The input is a 'Product Description' document for the 'Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform', detailing its 'Vision', 'Mission', 'three pillars', 'Personas', 'User Journeys', and 'Core Technological Infrastructure and Stack'. These are all unambiguous signals for a new product, platform, or service.

### Intent Statement
**Hestami is an AI-native operating system that unifies homeowners, landlords, service providers, and community associations into a single digital ecosystem designed to streamline maintenance, governance, and financial workflows. We aim to minimize friction by matching property needs with verified vendors while handling complex compliance and digital asset management autonomously where possible.**

Hestami serves homeowners, investors, contractors, and community associations by providing a unified platform for property maintenance, vendor management, and association compliance. The system integrates property asset registries, field service dispatch, financial transaction processing, and regulatory governance into one secure interface. It connects people requesting repairs with verified providers, manages digital assets, and handles administrative burdens, creating a seamless digital operating system for the real property and home services ecosystem.

**Who it serves:** Homeowner — Individual owners of a single residence or apartment unit who need maintenance and repairs.  
**Problem:** Hestami serves homeowners, investors, contractors, and community associations by providing a unified platform for property maintenance, vendor management, and association compliance. The system integrates property asset registries, field service dispatch, financial transaction processing, and regulatory governance into one secure interface. It connects people requesting repairs with verified providers, manages digital assets, and handles administrative burdens, creating a seamless digital operating system for the real property and home services ecosystem.

#### Confirmed Assumptions
- **DEC-1** — The product will follow a Three-Pillar Phasing Strategy to align product maturity with market readiness for each vertical (Owners, Vendors, Associations).
- **DEC-2** — AI automation will be integrated directly into the system of record rather than as a separate layer to ensure data consistency.
- **DEC-3** — The platform will prioritize offline-first mobile capabilities for field workers to ensure operational continuity.
- **DEC-4** — All external integrations must originate from the platform origin to maintain security and control.

#### Confirmed Constraints
- No direct client access to the system origin IP is permitted; all traffic must traverse Cloudflare.
- External partner integrations such as Nextdoor are initially out-of-scope.
- The platform must adhere to strict statutory deadlines for HOA notices and voting rules.
- Data for distinct communities must be logically and physically isolated to prevent cross-association data leakage.

#### Out of Scope
- Define primary revenue streams per pillar (subscription, transaction, or vendor fee)
- Determine threshold for AI agent autonomy versus human staff approval on vendor matching


## Functional Requirements (15 roots · 2327 total nodes · 893 atomic leaves)

### Release 1: Homeowner Core Services & Asset Digitalization (4 roots)
*This initial release empowers homeowners to seamlessly request and track home repair services from verified vendors. It also introduces the foundational capability for owners to digitalize their property assets by uploading photos and creating detailed profiles, setting the stage for a comprehensive property record. Basic dispute resolution and internal platform monitoring capabilities are also established to ensure a stable and reliable service from day one.*

### FR US-001 `[pending]`

**As a** Homeowner, **I want** Create a Service Call for repair need, **so that** Hestami CDM creates a new Work Order record linked to the property address.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-001** — Service call is persisted
    - _Measurable:_ POST /work-orders returns 201 and GET /work-orders/{id} returns the record within 1 second
  - **AC-002** — Service call metadata is captured
    - _Measurable:_ Work Order includes property address and issue description verbatim

_Status: pending · Priority: critical · Traces to: UJ-1, VOC-7, ENT-PROPERTY, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 0_

#### US-001-2 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** State Policy, **I want** Initialize Work Order Status to 'Open' on persistence, **so that** New Work Order enters the Workflow Engine in the 'Open' state immediately.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-002** — Status field value matches state machine initial state.
    - _Measurable:_ work_order.status === 'Open' immediately after commit

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, UJ-1 · Pass: 1_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Sets the initial state for the Workflow Engine (WF-2). This is an implementation commitment on state value.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0001, A-0002, A-0003

##### US-001-2-1 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** Database Engineer, **I want** Configure database column default for Work Order status, **so that** Inserted rows receive 'Open' status automatically from DB engine.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-002-DB** — DB default value enforces 'Open' on INSERT.
    - _Measurable:_ SELECT default FROM work_orders WHERE default IS NOT NULL; -> 'Open'

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Ensures persistence layer handles initialization independently of application logic, providing a safety net for the workflow engine.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0048, A-0049, A-0050

  - **US-001-2-1-001** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Define the literal string value for the default status, **so that** The database schema specifies 'Open' as the explicit default literal.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-001** — The column definition contains the literal string 'Open' (case-sensitive).
        - _Measurable:_ SELECT column_default FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'work_orders' AND column_name = 'status'; -> 'Open'

    _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ This child commits to the specific data value used by the DB engine, isolating the constant string definition from the DDL execution logic.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0170, A-0171, A-0172
    - **US-001-2-1-001-001** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** Database Engineer, **I want** Set status column default literal to 'Open', **so that** The 'status' column in 'work_orders' table returns 'Open' on insert without value.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-001-01** — Column default matches 'Open'.
          - _Measurable:_ SELECT column_default FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'work_orders' AND column_name = 'status'; -> 'Open'

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This child represents the atomic definition of the literal string value itself, the primary component of the schema commitment.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0379
    - **US-001-2-1-001-002** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** Workflow Engineer, **I want** Ensure default literal aligns with WF-2 state machine, **so that** The 'Open' default is interpreted correctly by the state machine logic in WF-2 as a valid initial state.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-002-01** — WF-2 configuration accepts 'Open' as start state.
          - _Measurable:_ WF-2 state machine transition rules allow 'Open' as start state

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This child ensures the schema value is semantically meaningful to the consuming workflow engine, preventing mismatch between database defaults and workflow logic.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0379
  - **US-001-2-1-002** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Configure DDL execution strategy for the default, **so that** ALTER TABLE statement is generated and deployed to the storage engine.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-002** — The DDL statement executes successfully without locking the table for longer than maintenance window.
        - _Measurable:_ ALTER TABLE work_orders ALTER COLUMN status SET DEFAULT 'Open'; -> executes without timeout > 30s

    _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, US-001-2-4 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ This child commits to the technical method (DDL vs Application logic) required to persist the default, complementing the App-side commit (US-001-2-4).

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0170, A-0171, A-0172
    - **FR-001** `[Tier B · pending]`
      **As a** Architecture, **I want** Select Online DDL as the schema change mechanism, **so that** Schema modifications avoid table locks during the maintenance window.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-003** — ALTER TABLE does not block reads or writes
          - _Measurable:_ Transaction isolation level permits concurrent access without deadlocks for duration > 10s

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: B · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This child binds the architectural decision on how to handle schema changes, distinguishing it from standard blocking DDL.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0380, A-0381, A-0382
      - **FR-001.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Invoke Online DDL syntax for schema changes, **so that** Schema modifications use the online mechanism to avoid exclusive table locks.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-001** — Schema change command includes online flag
            - _Measurable:_ "CONCURRENTLY" keyword or equivalent online flag is present in the executed DDL statement

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-001 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ This child directly implements the core architectural choice defined by the parent requirement. It binds the system to a specific SQL engine behavior that satisfies AC-003.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1522
      - **FR-001.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Preserve schema dependencies during execution, **so that** No constraints or triggers are invalidated or lost during the schema transition.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002** — No dangling references after schema change
            - _Measurable:_ Querying the dependency graph post-commit returns zero orphaned constraint references

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-001, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Online DDL often requires migrating dependent objects to temp schemas. This commitment ensures the integrity of the Work Order status machine (WF-2) remains intact during schema evolution.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1522
      - **FR-001.3** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Guard against lock contention before commit, **so that** Schema change aborts if blocking locks exceed safe duration thresholds.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — Aborts DDL if blocking locks exceed 10s
            - _Measurable:_ System returns error or aborts if active lock wait time exceeds 10 seconds prior to committing

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-001, FR-002 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ This child operationalizes AC-003. It prevents the DDL from holding locks during the 10s window by aborting early if contention prevents concurrent access, deferring the decision to FR-002 (timeout/failure).

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1522
    - **FR-002** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Constraint, **I want** Enforce maximum lock timeout of 30 seconds, **so that** Schema changes fail gracefully if they cannot complete within the maintenance window.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-002** — ALTER TABLE completes without locking > 30s
          - _Measurable:_ pg_locks.wait_time_ms <= 30000 for any DDL execution path

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This is a concrete implementation constraint ensuring availability requirements are met.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0380, A-0381, A-0382
      - **FR-002.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Engineer, **I want** Configure PostgreSQL lock_timeout parameter to 30000ms, **so that** Database engine enforces the 30s limit on DDL wait times.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002.1** — System uses lock_timeout setting of 30 seconds.
            - _Measurable:_ postgresql.lock_timeout = '30000ms' in connection pool config

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0799, A-0800
      - **FR-002.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Engineer, **I want** Implement rollback logic on lock_timeout error, **so that** Schema change fails gracefully and transaction is reverted.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002.2** — Transaction rolls back if lock_timeout is exceeded.
            - _Measurable:_ ROLLBACK status in transaction log when error_code is 55P03 or similar lock error

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, TECH-7 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Defining the 'fail gracefully' consequence as a concrete implementation decision (rollback).

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0799, A-0800
      - **FR-002.3** `[Tier B · pending]`
        **As a** Architect, **I want** Commit enforcement scope to DDL paths only, **so that** Lock timeout applies only to schema modification DDL statements.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

            - _Measurable:_ Configuration distinguishes between DDL and DML wait timeouts

        _Status: pending · Priority: medium · Tier: B · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 5_

  - **US-001-2-1-003** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Define migration backfill scope for existing rows, **so that** Existing NULL status values are not automatically backfilled by the default mechanism.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-003** — Insert-only enforcement: Existing rows retain their original status until explicitly updated or transitioned by WF-2.
        - _Measurable:_ SELECT COUNT(*) FROM work_orders WHERE status IS NULL AND created_at > 'migration_timestamp'; -> > 0 (NULLs persist on pre-migration)

    _Status: pending · Priority: medium · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, US-001-2-4 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ This child commits to the data integrity constraint of defaults applying only to new rows, distinguishing this scope from application-side validation.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0170, A-0171, A-0172
    - **FR-003-C01** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Migration Logic, **I want** Enforce retention of NULL status on pre-migration rows, **so that** Legacy work orders created before the migration timestamp maintain their NULL status value without automatic correction.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-004** — Legacy NULLs persist after migration.
          - _Measurable:_ COUNT(WO WHERE status IS NULL AND created_at < 'migration_timestamp') > 0 post-migration

        **Acceptance criteria:**

    - **FR-003-C02** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** New Row Defaults, **I want** Apply default literal 'Open' to newly created rows only, **so that** All work orders created after the migration timestamp utilize the defined default value for status.

      - **FR-003-C02-C01** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** DB Engineer, **I want** Configure database column default for Work Order status, **so that** Schema enforces 'Open' for inserts without explicit status value.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

      **As a** response_engine, **I want** Construct error message body string, **so that** HTTP 400 response body contains the string 'status: Open'.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-005-C2-2** — Error message text matches specification.
          - _Measurable:_ JSON.parse(res.body).error.message === 'status: Open' for all validation failures

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: US-001-2-1, US-001-2-4, US-001-2-3-C1 · Pass: 4_

    - **US-001-2-3-C2-3** `[Tier D · atomic]`

      **Acceptance criteria:**

          - _Measurable:_ res.headers['X-Stack-Trace'] === undefined && res.body.error !== {stack: ...} for all failures

      _Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: US-001-2-1, US-001-2-4 · Pass: 4_

  - **US-001-2-3-C3** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Enforce validation timing before database write, **so that** No INSERT query executes if validation throws.

    **Acceptance criteria:**

        - _Measurable:_ DB transaction is rolled back or never begins if schema.validate throws

    _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-2, US-001-2-4 · Pass: 3_

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0176, A-0177, A-0178
    - **US-001-2-3-C3-01** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** DB Operator, **I want** Initialize database transaction boundary only after schema validation passes, **so that** No database transaction is begun until the request payload is proven valid by the Zod schema..

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-001** — Transaction block begins only after schema validation.
          - _Measurable:_ Database connection transaction.begin() is called synchronously after schema.validate() returns without throwing.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0391, A-0392, A-0393
      - **US-001-2-3-C3-01-1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Request Handler, **I want** Validate request payload using Zod synchronously, **so that** Request proceeds or is rejected before any DB interaction.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-001-1** — Validation function returns success or throws error immediately.
            - _Measurable:_ await schema.validate() === success or throws within the same request lifecycle

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C1, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Validates the payload is the prerequisite step; distinct from the transaction management step.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0820, A-0821
      - **US-001-2-3-C3-01-2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Database Transaction Manager, **I want** Initiate transaction boundary context, **so that** Transaction context exists only if validation succeeded.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-001-2** — Transaction begin is called immediately after validation success check.
            - _Measurable:_ transaction.begin() executed immediately after AC-001-1 success without intervening async logic

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C1, WF-2, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Transaction management is a distinct architectural choice separate from input validation; ensures resource safety.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0820, A-0821
    - **US-001-2-3-C3-02** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Error Handler, **I want** Map schema validation exceptions to automatic transaction rollback signals, **so that** Any Zod validation error immediately triggers an abort of the current database transaction context..

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-002** — Transaction is rolled back on schema error.
          - _Measurable:_ If schema.validate() throws an Error, the database transaction.rollback() is executed within 10ms.

      _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C1, US-001-2-3-C2, TECH-8 · Pass: 4_

        **Acceptance criteria:**

      - **US-001-2-3-C3-02-002** `[Tier D · atomic]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004** — DBOS transaction context is aborted successfully
            - _Measurable:_ DBOS transaction state transitions from 'active' to 'aborted' and releases database locks

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: TECH-8, US-001-2-3-C3-01 · Pass: 5_

      - **US-001-2-3-C3-02-003** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-005** — Total time from validation exception to transaction abort < 10ms
            - _Measurable:_ Timestamp of error catch minus timestamp of DBOS abort call <= 10000000 nanoseconds

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C3-03 · Pass: 5_

      **As a** Architect, **I want** Configure transaction isolation level for Work Order creation paths, **so that** Reads and writes during the validation-to-commit window are isolated to prevent dirty reads from concurrent invalidation attempts..

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C3-03, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0824, A-0825
      - **US-001-2-3-C3-03.02** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** System Logic, **I want** Abort transaction on serialization anomaly, **so that** No partial state committed if two transactions conflict visibility-wise.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-005** — Transaction rolls back immediately upon serialization failure.
            - _Measurable:_ On PostgreSQL error code 40P01 (deadlock or serialization_failure), workflow state reverts to pre-commit

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C3-03, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-2-3-C3-03, WF-2 · Pass: 5_

##### US-001-2-4 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** System, **I want** Write 'Open' to Work Order status column on commit, **so that** Row in database contains 'Open' in status column.

    - _Measurable:_ SELECT status FROM work_orders WHERE id = :id; -> 'Open'

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0048, A-0049, A-0050

#### US-001-3 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** Input Validation, **I want** Validate request payload against Zod schema, **so that** Requests failing schema validation return 400 Bad Request.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003** — Invalid payloads reject with 400 status.
    - _Measurable:_ POST returns 400 if payload does not match schema definition

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: UJ-1, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 1_

##### US-001-3.1 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Parse incoming JSON body, **so that** Malformed JSON causes immediate rejection before schema check.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-004** — Malformed payloads reject with 400 status.
    - _Measurable:_ If JSON.parse() throws SyntaxError, response status is 400 and body is empty or structured error.

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-1, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ JSON parsing is a distinct, atomic step that must fail fast before any schema logic applies.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0051

##### US-001-3.2 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Enforce Zod schema types, **so that** Payload types match Zod definitions (string, number, enum, etc.).

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-005** — Type mismatches trigger 400 rejection.
    - _Measurable:_ schema.parse(payload) throws ZodError with type mismatch for every non-matching field.

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-1, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Type safety is a primary responsibility of the validation layer to prevent downstream type errors.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0051

##### US-001-3.3 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Enforce required field presence, **so that** Missing mandatory fields trigger 400 rejection.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-006** — Required fields present or validation fails.
    - _Measurable:_ Zod Error includes 'instance' of 'REQUIRED' for every missing key in the schema.

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-1, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Required fields are a subset of schema logic that can be isolated for testing.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0051

##### US-001-3.4 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Log validation failure event, **so that** Validation errors recorded in audit trail for compliance.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-007** — Error logged to audit trail immediately after rejection.
    - _Measurable:_ ENT-AUDIT-LOG contains entry with timestamp, error code, and payload snippet (anonymized).

_Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-1, US-001-4 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ This aligns with sibling US-001-4 which mandates logging creation events; validation failures also require audit trail coverage.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0051

##### US-001-3.5 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Return structured 400 response, **so that** HTTP 400 sent with standardized error message.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-008** — Response body contains 'statusCode' and 'message'.
    - _Measurable:_ Response JSON includes 'statusCode': 400 and 'message' describing validation error.

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-1, ENT-WORK-ORDER · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Client-facing error format is an atomic action required to satisfy the 'return 400' acceptance criterion.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0051

##### FR-WO-FK-2 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** API Developer, **I want** Validate property_id existence against ENT-PROPERTY registry before persistence, **so that** Request rejected with 400 Bad Request before database write.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-VAL-01** — API endpoint rejects payload without existing property reference.
    - _Measurable:_ HTTP 400 response sent when work_order.property_id is missing or not found in ENT-PROPERTY lookup

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-3, UJ-1 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Extends the architectural commitment to the API edge, aligning with sibling US-001-3 (Schema Validation) but focusing on FK integrity rather than just field presence.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0161, A-0162

  - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** API Implementation Strategy, **I want** Execute synchronous lookup of ENT-PROPERTY registry before write, **so that** System validates property existence in real-time before accepting work order data.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-001** — Registry lookup response is awaited before commit.
        - _Measurable:_ Lookup call latency < write timeout for every request with property_id field

    _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: US-001-3, FR-WO-FK-1 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ Defines the architectural timing strategy (Synchronous vs Asynchronous) required to meet the 'before database write' constraint.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0345, A-0346
    - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-1** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** API Error Handler, **I want** Compose HTTP 400 Bad Request response when registry lookup fails with 404 Not Found, **so that** Client receives immediate rejection without DB write or validation proceeding.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-1.1** — Status code 400 returned for unknown property_id
          - _Measurable:_ http_response_status_code === 400 && error_body.property_id === request.property_id && error_body.reason === 'ENTITY_NOT_FOUND'

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: AC-001, FR-WO-FK-2-C-02, FR-WO-FK-2-C-03 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ Defines the specific error payload strategy when the synchronous lookup fails to find an entity, aligning with sibling requirements for immediate rejection and actionable guidance.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0684, A-0685, A-0686
      - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-1-D-1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** API Error Handler, **I want** Compose and send HTTP 400 Bad Request response with property_id and reason, **so that** Client receives immediate rejection with specific error body and status code.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-001** — Status code 400 returned for unknown property_id
            - _Measurable:_ http_response_status_code === 400 && error_body.property_id === request.property_id && error_body.reason === 'ENTITY_NOT_FOUND'

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: AC-001 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Operation is atomic; response composition for a specific error condition is a single testable unit that defines the boundary of the failure behavior.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1309, A-1310, A-1311
    - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-2** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Transaction Consistency Enforcer, **I want** Rollback DB transaction immediately if property write fails after successful lookup, **so that** No partial state of Work Order or Property is persisted on the database.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-1.2** — Database transaction status is ROLLED_BACK
          - _Measurable:_ db_transaction.rollback_completed === true && db_transaction.write_fails === true

      _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: AC-001, FR-WO-FK-1 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ Ensures the synchronous lookup commitment does not introduce data consistency hazards by enforcing atomicity between read and write phases.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0684, A-0685, A-0686
      - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-2.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** execute database transaction rollback command upon write failure detection, **so that** Database transaction state is reverted to savepoint or initial state.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-1.2.1** — Database rollback status confirms success.
            - _Measurable:_ db_transaction.rollback_completed === true && db_transaction.write_fails === true

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-1, FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-3, FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-4 · Pass: 5_

      - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-2.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** State Validator, **I want** verify no partial state persists for Work Order or Property entities after rollback, **so that** Database query confirms zero partial rows for affected entities.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

            - _Measurable:_ count(ent_work_order WHERE status = 'PARTIAL') === 0 && count(ent_property WHERE status = 'PARTIAL') === 0

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-3, ENT-WORK-ORDER, ENT-PROPERTY · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1312, A-1313
      - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-2.3** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Audit Logger, **I want** emit failure event and rollback log entry for compliance audit trail, **so that** Compliant audit record is generated without persisting the failed state.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

    - **FR-WO-FK-2-C-01-3** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Property State Validator, **I want** Reject requests where ENT-PROPERTY status does not equal 'ACTIVE', **so that** System validates that the existing property is eligible for new Work Order assignment.

##### FR-AUD-1.3 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** Implementation commitment, **I want** Include cryptographic hash of event data in log record, **so that** Data integrity can be verified against stored hash.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003** — Stored hash matches live record content.
    - _Measurable:_ verify(hash(audit_entry_data)) === stored_hash_value

_Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Concrete implementation choice for integrity verification (Tier C) that binds the commitment to an algorithmic process.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0163, A-0164, A-0165, A-0166

  - **FR-AUD-1.3.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** Security Architect, **I want** Commit to SHA-256 hashing algorithm, **so that** Hash function produces a standard 64-character hex digest.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-004** — Hash function output length is 64 hex characters.
        - _Measurable:_ length(hex_digest) === 64

    _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3, ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ Selecting a specific hash algorithm is an implementation choice; SHA-256 is the industry standard for audit integrity and satisfies collision resistance requirements without needing further scope decomposition.
    - **FR-AUD-1.3.1.1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** Algorithm Selector, **I want** Invoke crypto.createHash('sha256') in Bun runtime, **so that** Algorithm name is hardcoded to 'sha256' in the implementation.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-005** — The hashing function is instantiated with the name 'sha256'.
          - _Measurable:_ crypto.createHash(name).digest('hex') === <result> and name === 'sha256'

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.1 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ Breaks the commitment to SHA-256 into the concrete implementation action of selecting the algorithm string.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0692
    - **FR-AUD-1.3.1.2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** Format Enforcer, **I want** Convert binary digest to lowercase hexadecimal string, **so that** Persisted value matches standard 64-character hex digest format.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-006** — Digest output is a string containing only lowercase a-f and 0-9 characters with length 64.
          - _Measurable:_ hexdigest.length === 64 && /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/.test(hexdigest)

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.1 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ Enforces the specific output formatting (lowercase hex) required to satisfy the acceptance criterion regarding length and standard format.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0692
  - **FR-AUD-1.3.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** Backend Engineer, **I want** Hash full payload before persisting, **so that** Hash covers all mutable fields except immutable system fields.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-005** — Hash input includes all event fields.
        - _Measurable:_ hash_input == JSON.stringify(full_record)

    _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3, FR-AUD-1.4 · Pass: 3_

    - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Compute hash on the write thread before transaction commit., **so that** Record never persists without computed hash..

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-006** — Hash value present in row before commit transaction completes.
          - _Measurable:_ Hash computed in application memory and persisted in DB column prior to SQL COMMIT

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2 · Pass: 4_

      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.2.1** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-101** — Hash output matches standard length and format.
            - _Measurable:_ hash_string.length === 64 && hash_string === hex_encode(sha256(input_bytes))

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2.2, FR-AUD-1.3.2.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1326, A-1327
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.2.2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** System, **I want** Persist hash result in record storage layer within transaction block, **so that** Record row contains valid hash value in its dedicated column at commit time.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-102** — Row contains hash value prior to COMMIT statement.
            - _Measurable:_ SELECT hash_column FROM record WHERE id = row_id AND hash_column IS NOT NULL AND hash_value = computed_hash

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2.2, FR-AUD-1.3.2.3 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ This child defines the terminal leaf action of writing the computed value to the database, aligning with the persistence commitment of the parent.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1326, A-1327
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.2.3** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** System Architect, **I want** Isolate write thread context from concurrent reads during hashing, **so that** No read/write data race occurs during the window between hash calculation and commit.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-103** — No stale data read is returned while hashing.
            - _Measurable:_ write_thread_lock_state === 'active' during compute_hash(); read_thread_state === 'blocked' or 'safe_snapshot'

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2.2, FR-AUD-1.3.2.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ This child addresses the threading constraint implied by 'write thread' in the parent, committing to a system-side restriction (locking) to ensure correctness.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1326, A-1327
    - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.3** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Persist hash in a dedicated column within the record storage layer., **so that** Hash is durable and retrievable for verification..

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-007** — Hash column is populated in the storage engine (PG or SeaweedFS).
          - _Measurable:_ Schema includes dedicated 'audit_checksum' column for each record entity

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2 · Pass: 4_

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0693
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.3.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Ensure schema defines 'audit_checksum' column for all applicable record entities, **so that** Schema DDL includes the dedicated column before any record persists.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-007.1** — Database schema query returns the 'audit_checksum' column for entity tables.
            - _Measurable:_ SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'work_orders' AND column_name = 'audit_checksum' AND table_schema = 'public' MUST return true.

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2, TECH-8, TECH-14 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1328, A-1329
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.3.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Guarantee atomic write of hash alongside business data, **so that** A record is never committed to the storage engine without a populated hash value.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-007.2** — Transaction aborts if hash is missing or null.
            - _Measurable:_ IF transaction_status = 'COMMITTED' THEN SELECT audit_checksum FROM target_entity WHERE primary_key = <id> MUST return a non-null hash value.

It commits to the transactional invariant that data integrity is preserved by ensuring the hash is part of the committed unit of work, bridging the compute (FR-AUD-1.3.2.2) and storage (FR-AUD-1.3.2.3) layers.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1328, A-1329
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.3.3** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2.3, WF-3, TECH-4 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1328, A-1329
      - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.3.4** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Adapt persistence logic to storage engine specifics (PG vs SeaweedFS), **so that** Storage layer code handles column population and retrieval correctly for both supported engines.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.3.2.3, TECH-8, TECH-14 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ The AC mentions 'PG or SeaweedFS', implying engine adaptation. This child commits to the logic that abstracts the hash storage mechanism so it works regardless of which engine is deployed (addressing the existing open question A-0166 by ensuring compatibility rather than selecting one).

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1328, A-1329
    - **FR-AUD-1.3.2.1** `[downgraded]`

##### FR-AUD-1.4 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** Implementation commitment, **I want** Log entry payload includes actor identity and session token, **so that** Every event is attributable to a specific user/action context.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-004** — Log record contains user_id and session_id fields.
    - _Measurable:_ audit_entry.schema.contains(user_id, session_id)

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG, TECH-8 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Defines the scope of the event payload, ensuring accountability without requiring external authority citation.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0163, A-0164, A-0165, A-0166

  - **FR-AUD-1.4.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** Backend Service, **I want** Resolve user_id from Auth Context (Better-Auth), **so that** Every audit log record contains the resolved user identifier from the active auth session.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-004.1** — Log record user_id matches the current authenticated user
        - _Measurable:_ log.user_id === auth_context.claims.sub

    _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG, TECH-11 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ Decomposes the requirement into the specific source of truth for the user identity. Commits to using TECH-11 (Better-Auth) as the provider for user resolution, ensuring consistency across the platform.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0349
    - **FR-AUD-1.4.1.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Authentication Handler, **I want** Extract 'sub' claim from Better-Auth session object in middleware, **so that** Authentication service returns valid session token with user claims.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-005** — Extracted token claims are validated before log write
          - _Measurable:_ auth_context.claims.sub !== null && auth_context.is_verified === true

      _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-11 · Pass: 4_

**As a** Architectural choice, **I want** Enforce append-only storage strategy for audit records, **so that** Log records cannot be deleted or overwritten by application writes.

_Status: downgraded · Priority: critical · Traces to: COMP-1, ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 3_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1419, A-1420, A-1421
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-1-CH-003** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Backend Engineer, **I want** Return HTTP 409 Conflict for PUT requests to existing object IDs, **so that** API clients are immediately rejected from overwriting existing audit objects.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — PUT to existing object ID returns 409.
            - _Measurable:_ http_response_code(put_request(object_id)) === 409

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: AC-002b-1, TECH-9 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1419, A-1420, A-1421
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-1-CH-004** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Storage Engineer, **I want** Enforce WORM retention period matching system audit policy, **so that** Locked objects persist for the full retention duration before being eligible for unlock/deletion.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004** — Objects remain locked for the defined retention window.
            - _Measurable:_ lock_expiry_timestamp === object_creation_timestamp + retention_period_days

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: AC-002b-1, A-0163 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1419, A-1420, A-1421
    - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-2** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Database Architect, **I want** Enforce Row-Level Security (RLS) policies that deny UPDATE and DELETE on audit tables., **so that** No user, including standard application service accounts, can modify existing audit rows via DML..

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-002b-2** — RLS policy denies DML on audit records.
          - _Measurable:_ Any UPDATE/DELETE statement on audit tables returns permission denied or fails at the database level.

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8 · Pass: 4_

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0753

        **Acceptance criteria:**

            - _Measurable:_ SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename LIKE 'audit%' > 0;

      - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-2-2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Security Engineer, **I want** Configure role permissions to deny UPDATE/DELETE for application service accounts., **so that** Application service accounts lack DML rights on audit tables..

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002** — App service accounts cannot UPDATE/DELETE audit rows.
            - _Measurable:_ GRANT SELECT ON audit_* TO app_svc; DENY UPDATE, DELETE ON audit_* TO app_svc;

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8, ENT-ROLE · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1422, A-1423, A-1424
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-2-3** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — No trigger conflicts exist.
            - _Measurable:_ SELECT 1 FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname LIKE 'audit%' AND tgtype != 'f' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname IN ('app_svc') AND rolinherit = false);

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8, FR-AUD-1.2.2-2-1 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1422, A-1423, A-1424
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-2-4** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004** — Policy modification audit log entries exist.

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8, ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 5_

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.2-3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** System Operator, **I want** Generate and persist a SHA-256 hash of audit content upon initial write., **so that** Content tampering is detectable by recomputing the hash against the stored value..

          - _Measurable:_ If user.ROLE != 'ADMIN' then REJECT_TRANSACTION

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.3.2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Validate immutability schema constraint, **so that** Reject write request if immutability flag is missing or invalid.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-002c-2** — Request fails validation if schema constraint on audit log is violated.
          - _Measurable:_ If AUDIT_LOG.immutability_flag is NULL or TYPE != BOOLEAN then REJECT_TRANSACTION

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.3.3** `[Tier D · atomic]`

      _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: TECH-8, FR-AUD-1.2.5 · Pass: 4_

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.5-D3** `[Tier D · atomic]`

      **Acceptance criteria:**

          - _Measurable:_ abs(log_entry.timestamp - server_time) <= 0.1

      _Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG, FR-AUD-1.2.5 · Pass: 4_

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0757, A-0758, A-0759

          - _Measurable:_ SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_log WHERE event_id = log_entry.id === 1

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0757, A-0758, A-0759

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.2** `[Tier C · pending]`

      **Acceptance criteria:**

        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Filter eligible records where retention_expiry_date <= now, **so that** Exactly the set of records ready for mutation is identified for processing.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — Filtered record count matches query result set.

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        **Acceptance criteria:**

        _Status: pending · Priority: medium · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-7, WF-5 · Pass: 5_

    - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.3** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Backend Engineer, **I want** Ensure atomic update to immutability flag to prevent race conditions, **so that** No window exists where an entry is simultaneously immutable and non-immutable.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-003** — Update operation uses transactional locking on audit entry.
          - _Measurable:_ Transaction completes with isolation level SERIALIZABLE and writes ENT-AUDIT-LOG.is_immutable = false

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8, A-0164 · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This child commits to the technical constraint (atomicity) required to satisfy the Tier B policy. It rules out non-atomic soft deletes that could cause inconsistency.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0797, A-0798
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Enforce row-level locking on ENT-AUDIT-LOG records during update, **so that** No other transaction can modify the locked row until the current transaction commits or rolls back.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003-A** — Lock acquired and released within the same transaction block.
            - _Measurable:_ pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(...) is invoked before write on ENT-AUDIT-LOG.row_id

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Defines the granularity of concurrency control. Row-level locking minimizes impact on other concurrent reads/writes, fulfilling the requirement to prevent race conditions on specific audit entries.
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Configure transaction isolation level to SERIALIZABLE, **so that** Database guarantees consistency and prevents dirty reads/writes during the update sequence.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003-B** — Transaction commits with SERIALIZABLE isolation enabled.
            - _Measurable:_ SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE is active before lock acquisition.

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-8 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Locking alone is insufficient without appropriate isolation. SERIALIZABLE prevents phantom reads and serialization failures, ensuring the atomicity of the immutability flag update.
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Verify `is_immutable` state immediately after write, **so that** The system is confident the flag is in the intended state (false) post-commit.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003-C** — Consistent read of ENT-AUDIT-LOG.is_immutable matches written value.
            - _Measurable:_ SELECT is_immutable FROM ENT-AUDIT-LOG WHERE id = $target_id RETURNING true.

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.1, FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.2 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Leaf operation validating the atomic result of the update. Ensures the 'no window' constraint is met by verifying state consistency.
      - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.4** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Implement retry logic on deadlock detection exceptions, **so that** System recovers from contention without data loss or indefinite blocking.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003-D** — Deadlock detected triggers retry of the update operation.
            - _Measurable:_ EXCEPTION DEADLOCK DETECTED results in transaction rollback and retry after sleep.

        _Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.1, FR-AUD-1.2.4.3.2, FR-AUD-1.2.4.4 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Atomic operations in multi-threaded environments must handle conflicts. This leaf operation defines the failure mode recovery to maintain system stability.
    - **FR-AUD-1.2.4.4** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** Data Store, **I want** Clear `is_immutable` column value to false, **so that** Record is no longer protected from mutation after retention expiry.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-004** — Column value changes from true to false.
          - _Measurable:_ SELECT is_immutable FROM ENT-AUDIT-LOG WHERE id = target_id AND retention_expiry_date <= now() RETURNING is_immutable

      _Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This is the atomic leaf operation that executes the enforcement of the immutability release. It is testable individually against the database state.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0797, A-0798
### FR US-002 `[pending]`

**As a** Homeowner, **I want** View Work Order status updates, **so that** User sees status transition from Open to Scheduled or Completed.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003** — Status updates propagate
    - _Measurable:_ Status change visible on Dashboard within 30 seconds of technician input
  - **AC-004** — Notification delivered
    - _Measurable:_ ENT-NOTIFY-LOG records successful delivery to user's channel within 5 minutes

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Traces to: UJ-2, WF-2, WF-6, ENT-NOTIFY-LOG · Pass: 0_

#### US-002-1.1 `[Tier B · pending]`

**As a** State Machine Enforcer, **I want** Validate status transitions against the Work Order state machine schema, **so that** Only defined state transitions (e.g., Open -> Scheduled) are allowed.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003.1** — State transitions adhere to the workflow graph.
    - _Measurable:_ Workflow engine rejects invalid state transitions at write time

_Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: B · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 1_

_Decomposition rationale:_ This child binds the system behavior to the governing workflow rules defined in WF-2, ensuring the transition promise is honored as a policy choice.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0004, A-0005, A-0006

##### US-002-1.1-C1 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** Workflow Architect, **I want** Define transition graph schema version loaded into DBOS, **so that** Workflow engine graph schema loaded matches WF-2 specification.

**Acceptance criteria:**

    - _Measurable:_ graph_schema_version === WF-2_latest_version

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 2_

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0167, A-0168

  - **US-002-1.1-C1-D1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
    **As a** State Manager, **I want** Write active schema version identifier to DBOS state store, **so that** Runtime config contains the version string.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-D1** — Version field is populated in the active configuration record

    _Status: atomic · Priority: high · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 3_

  - **US-002-1.1-C1-D2** `[Tier D · atomic]`

    **Acceptance criteria:**

    _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 3_

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0350
  - **US-002-1.1-C1-D3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
    **As a** Context Injector, **I want** Expose version variable to DBOS execution context, **so that** Workflow functions can read the active version.

    **Acceptance criteria:**

        - _Measurable:_ runtime.context.has('graph_schema_version') === true

    _Status: atomic · Priority: medium · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-2 · Pass: 3_

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0350
##### US-002-1.1-C2 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** DB Enforcer, **I want** Enforce atomic validation at write time within DBOS transaction, **so that** Database transaction rolls back if transition check fails.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003.1.2** — Invalid transition triggers rollback of work order state.
    - _Measurable:_ write_operation_success === false IF transition_is_valid === false

_Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, TECH-7 · Pass: 2_

  - **US-002-1.1-C2-2** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** System, **I want** Enforce DBOS transaction boundary around write, **so that** Write operation is atomic with respect to DBOS workflow.

    - **US-002-1.1-C2-2-01** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**

      - **US-002-1.1-C2-2-01-02** `[Tier C · pending]`

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: US-002-1.1-C2-2-01 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1340, A-1341
      - **US-002-1.1-C2-2-01-03** `[Tier C · pending]`

  - **US-002-1.1-C2-3** `[Tier C · pending]`

        - **AC-003.1** — State restored matches snapshot from current transaction boundary
          - _Measurable:_ restored_state_hash === snapshot_hash_taken_before_transition

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, TECH-7 · Pass: 4_

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-7 · Pass: 5_

            - _Measurable:_ hash_algorithm == 'SHA-256'

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: TECH-9 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Industry standard for integrity verification; ensures that state restoration is not subject to algorithmic rollback attacks.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1345, A-1346, A-1347
      - **US-002-1.1-C2-3.1.D1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Workflow Orchestrator, **I want** Capture state snapshot hash at transition start, **so that** Hash available for rollback comparison.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — Snapshot hash computed at transition start.
            - _Measurable:_ snapshot_hash == calculate_hash(current_state_snapshot)

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: AC-003.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Atomic action required to satisfy the parent acceptance criterion that restored state matches the snapshot.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1345, A-1346, A-1347
      - **US-002-1.1-C2-3.1.D2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Recovery System, **I want** Validate restored state hash on failure, **so that** Hash match confirms rollback integrity.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004** — Restored state hash matches snapshot hash.
            - _Measurable:_ restored_state_hash === snapshot_hash_taken_before_transition

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: AC-003.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Core verification step of the requirement. Ensures data integrity before committing the rollback.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1345, A-1346, A-1347
      - **US-002-1.1-C2-3.1.D3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Data Recovery Engine, **I want** Restore only mutated rows to previous version, **so that** Non-mutated rows remain untouched during rollback.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-005** — Restored rows are limited to mutation delta.
            - _Measurable:_ restored_rows == delta(current_state, snapshot)

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: AC-003.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1345, A-1346, A-1347
    - **US-002-1.1-C2-3.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** System State Manager, **I want** Persist previous state snapshot before transition, **so that** Previous state is available for restoration without side effects.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-003.2** — State snapshot exists in storage before transition validation
          - _Measurable:_ snapshot_exists === true before transition_is_valid check

      - **FR-ACCT-1.1.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Utilize DBOS internal state store for snapshot persistence, **so that** Snapshot remains accessible within the same transaction context without external I/O latency.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004.2** — Snapshot read latency does not exceed 50ms
            - _Measurable:_ Timestamp(snapshot_read_time) - Timestamp(snapshot_creation_time) <= 0.05s for every failed transition

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, TECH-7 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1348, A-1349, A-1350
      - **FR-ACCT-1.1.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Serialize only workflow version deltas into the snapshot, **so that** Snapshot size is minimized by excluding ephemeral data (sessions, preferences).

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004.3** — Snapshot excludes non-workflow entities
            - _Measurable:_ Snapshot payload does not contain fields from ENT-SESSION-TOKEN or ENT-USER

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, TECH-8 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Prevents data bloat and ensures rollback restores only the specific workflow state version relevant to WF-2.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1348, A-1349, A-1350
      - **FR-ACCT-1.1.3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Validate snapshot signature on restore attempt, **so that** Restored state integrity is verified before applying to the transaction.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004.4** — Hash verification passes for valid snapshot
            - _Measurable:_ SHA-256(snapshot_file_contents) === Stored_Hash in metadata_index

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: ENT-AUDIT-LOG, TECH-8 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Provides the atomic verification required for AC-003.2; ensures corruption does not compromise state recovery.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1348, A-1349, A-1350
      - **FR-ACCT-1.1.4** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** CAM operator, **I want** Expire snapshots after workflow version update, **so that** Storage costs are managed by retaining snapshots for a fixed window before deletion.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-004.5** — Snapshot garbage collection completes within scheduled batch
            - _Measurable:_ Garbage_collected_snapshots === Total_snapshots_since_workflow_update for every expired window

        _Status: pending · Priority: medium · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-2, ENT-AUDIT-LOG · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Ensures storage sustainability; A-0704 dictates no notifications are sent during deletion.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-1348, A-1349, A-1350
    - **US-002-1.1-C2-3.3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
      **As a** System, **I want** Invoke DBOS rollback hook on failure, **so that** Previous state is restored immediately after validation failure.

      **Acceptance criteria:**

##### US-002-1.1-C3 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** Error Handler, **I want** Surface rejection error payload to client application, **so that** Client receives 400 Bad Request with human-readable error message.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0167, A-0168

##### US-002-1.2-01 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** DB Writer, **I want** Write status value to ENT-WORK-ORDER.status, **so that** ENT-WORK-ORDER.status contains new value upon transaction completion.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-004** — Row status matches new value at commit.
    - _Measurable:_ SELECT status FROM ENT-WORK-ORDER WHERE id = :id AND status = :new_value returns true

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: ENT-WORK-ORDER, WF-2 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Core persistence operation required to satisfy the functional update need. This is the terminal data modification step.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0052, A-0053

##### US-002-1.2-02 `[Tier D · atomic]`

**As a** Transaction Manager, **I want** Commit or rollback DB transaction on workflow signal, **so that** Transaction scope completes without indefinite locking.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-005** — Transaction ends within 5 seconds of start.
    - _Measurable:_ EXTRACT (NOW() - transaction_start) FROM db_transaction_logs WHERE status = 'COMMITTED' OR status = 'ROLLEDBACK'

_Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: TECH-8, WF-2, US-002-1.2 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Ensures atomicity and availability (AC-003.2). This is the implementation commitment defining the transaction boundary and timeout constraint.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0052, A-0053

#### US-002-1.3 `[Tier C · pending]`

    - _Measurable:_ ENT-NOTIFY-LOG record exists within 5 minutes of user action

  - **FR-ACCT-1.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
    **As a** Reconnection Backoff, **I want** Initiate reconnection attempt with exponential backoff on failure, **so that** Client reconnects to server after defined delay sequences.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-102** — First reconnect attempt occurs after 1s delay.
        - _Measurable:_ Connection attempt scheduled at timestamp = failure_timestamp + 1000ms

    _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-9, ENT-SESSION-TOKEN · Pass: 3_

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0183, A-0184
    - **FR-ACCT-1.2.1** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Configuration Commitment, **I want** Initialize retry delay to 1000ms upon first failure, **so that** First reconnect attempt waits 1s before scheduling.

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-1.2.1-A** — Delay is exactly 1s on first failure.
          - _Measurable:_ scheduled_timestamp === failure_timestamp + 1000ms for attempt #1

      _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-9, ENT-SESSION-TOKEN · Pass: 4_

      _Decomposition rationale:_ This commits to the specific implementation value of the base delay parameter. It is a concrete engineering choice required to satisfy the parent AC.

      _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0406, A-0407, A-0408
      - **FR-ACCT-1.2.1.1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Client, **I want** Detect failure event and validate it is the first attempt, **so that** System confirms this is the initial failure (attempt #1) before applying the initial delay.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-001** — Failure counter increments to 1 upon this event.
            - _Measurable:_ failure_counter === 1 and previous_failure_count === 0

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-9, ENT-SESSION-TOKEN · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ Validating the failure count ensures the 1000ms delay is applied only to the first failure, distinguishing it from subsequent retries handled in FR-ACCT-1.2.2.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0840, A-0841
      - **FR-ACCT-1.2.1.2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Client, **I want** Apply constant delay value of 1000ms, **so that** Retry timer is configured with the initial baseline delay of 1000 milliseconds.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002** — Calculated delay equals 1000ms for the first attempt.
            - _Measurable:_ delay_value_ms === 1000

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-ACCT-1.2.1, FR-ACCT-1.2.2 · Pass: 5_

        _Decomposition rationale:_ This child defines the specific implementation constant for the initial backoff, separating the base value from the growth multipliers in FR-ACCT-1.2.2.

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0840, A-0841
      - **FR-ACCT-1.2.1.3** `[Tier D · atomic]`
        **As a** Client, **I want** Schedule initial retry connection attempt, **so that** System initiates the first WebSocket reconnection after the 1000ms delay.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-003** — Retry event fires after 1000ms from failure.
            - _Measurable:_ timer.fire_timestamp - failure_timestamp === 1000

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: FR-ACCT-1.2.1.2, FR-ACCT-1.2.5 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0840, A-0841
    - **FR-ACCT-1.2.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
      **As a** Algorithm Commitment, **I want** Apply 2.0 multiplier to delay for each subsequent failure, **so that** Retry delay grows exponentially (1s, 2s, 4s, ...).

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-1.2.2-A** — Delay for attempt N = Delay(N-1) * 2.
          - _Measurable:_ delay_attempt[2] === delay_attempt[1] * 2 && delay_attempt[3] === delay_attempt[2] * 2

      _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-9, UJ-12 · Pass: 4_

      - **FR-ACCT-1.2.2.1** `[Tier C · pending]`

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-1.2.2.1-A** — Retrieved delay value matches current stored session state.
            - _Measurable:_ delay_read_value === session_storage['backoff_delay'] at calculation start

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: WF-9 · Pass: 5_

      - **FR-ACCT-1.2.2.2** `[Tier C · pending]`
        **As a** Logic Operator, **I want** Apply 2.0 multiplier to retrieved delay value, **so that** New delay value represents exactly 2x the previous delay.

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-1.2.2.2-A** — Computed delay value equals previous value multiplied by 2.0.
            - _Measurable:_ calculated_delay === delay_read_value * 2.0

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-ACCT-1.2.1 · Pass: 5_

        _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0842, A-0843

        _Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-ACCT-1.2.5 · Pass: 5_

    - **FR-ACCT-1.2.3** `[Tier C · pending]`

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-1.2.3-A** — Delay does not exceed 60s.
          - _Measurable:_ delay_attempt[N] <= 60000ms for all N

        **Acceptance criteria:**
          - **AC-002** — Delay calculation respects the 60s ceiling.
            - _Measurable:_ Math.min(current_delay, 60000) === scheduled_delay

        _Status: pending · Priority: critical · Tier: C · Traces to: FR-ACCT-1.2.2 · Pass: 5_

        **Acceptance criteria:**

            - _Measurable:_ session_storage.backoff_delay <= 60000

##### US-002-1.4.3 `[Tier C · pending]`

**As a** CAM operator, **I want** Measure delta between DB commit time and client render time, **so that** End-to-end latency is verified to be under the 30 second threshold.

**Acceptance criteria:**
  - **AC-003.3.3** — Total latency from server commit to user screen render is logged and verified.
    - _Measurable:_ render_timestamp - commit_timestamp <= 30000 milliseconds

_Status: pending · Priority: high · Tier: C · Traces to: UJ-2, TECH-4 · Pass: 2_

_Decomposition rationale:_ Verification commitment ensuring the SLA defined in the parent AC is met.

_Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0056, A-0057

  - **FR-002-1.4.3.1** `[Tier D · atomic]`
    **As a** Backend Instrumentation, **I want** Capture atomic commit timestamp from database transaction, **so that** Backend commit timestamp is available for latency calculation.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-001** — Commit timestamp is recorded at the exact moment of DB commit.
        - _Measurable:_ timestamp === transaction_commit_timestamp

    _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-2, TECH-4, US-002-1.4.1, US-002-1.4.2 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ Backend instrumentation is the atomic source for the first half of the latency calculation; requires capture at commit time.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0185, A-0186, A-0187
  - **FR-002-1.4.3.2** `[Tier D · atomic]`
    **As a** Frontend Instrumentation, **I want** Capture render timestamp from client side, **so that** Client-side render completion timestamp is available.

    **Acceptance criteria:**
      - **AC-002** — Render timestamp is captured upon DOM rendering completion.
        - _Measurable:_ timestamp === performance.now()_at_render_complete

    _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: UJ-2, TECH-4, US-002-1.4.2 · Pass: 3_

    _Decomposition rationale:_ Frontend instrumentation is the atomic source for the second half of the latency calculation; relies on TECH-4 SvelteKit capabilities.

    _Surfaced assumptions:_ A-0185, A-0186, A-0187

      **Acceptance criteria:**
        - **AC-101** — Generated ID is unique and sortable by time.
          - _Measurable:_ uuid_version(id) === 7 AND max(id) - min(id) >= duration_of_service_in_seconds

        _Status: atomic · Priority: critical · Tier: D · Traces to: WF-9, WF-15 · Pass: 5_
